content: Neuromodulation candidate evaluation requires multidisciplinary assessment. DBS Parkinson's: confirm idiopathic PD (UK Brain Bank criteria), document levodopa response (>30% UPDRS-III improvement in acute challenge), assess motor complications (wearing-off, dyskinesia duration/severity), cognitive screening (MoCA ≥26 preferred, formal neuropsychological testing if borderline), psychiatric evaluation (exclude active depression, psychosis, impulse control disorders), MRI brain (exclude significant atrophy, vascular burden, structural lesions). Essential tremration: quantify tremor severity ( Bain scale), exclude reversible causes (thyroid, medications). Dystonia: genetic testing if early-onset (DYT1), assess distribution (generalized/segmental better candidates than focal). TMS depression: confirm MDD diagnosis (DSM-5), document treatment resistance (failure ≥1-2 adequate antidepressant trials), exclude contraindications (metallic implants within 30cm of coil, cochlear implant, history of seizures), screen for bipolar disorder (risk of mania). Outcome measures: UPDRS-III for PD, Fahn-Tolosa-Marin for tremor, Burke-Fahn-Marsden for dystonia, HAM-D/MADRS for depression, seizure frequency for epilepsy. Realistic expectations crucial—DBS improves symptoms but doesn't halt disease progression.
